title: Advanced World History O/X
description: Key turning points in world history

cards.prompt: The French Revolution began in 1789, marked by the storming of the Bastille.
cards.explanation: The storming of the Bastille on 14 July 1789 is the symbolic start of the French Revolution.

cards.prompt: World War I began in 1914, triggered by the assassination in Sarajevo.
cards.explanation: The assassination of Archduke Franz Ferdinand in Sarajevo helped ignite WWI in 1914.

cards.prompt: The Industrial Revolution first began in Japan in the late 18th century.
cards.explanation: The Industrial Revolution began in Britain in the late 18th century, not Japan.

cards.prompt: The Declaration of Independence of the United States was adopted in 1776.
cards.explanation: The U.S. Declaration of Independence was adopted on 4 July 1776.

cards.prompt: The Roman Empire was founded in the 20th century.
cards.explanation: The Roman Empire dates to antiquity (traditionally from 27 BC), not the 20th century.

cards.prompt: The Cold War was a period of geopolitical tension mainly between the United States and the Soviet Union.
cards.explanation: The Cold War was the post-WWII rivalry between the U.S.-led West and the Soviet-led East.
World War II ended in 1945 after beginning in 1939.

cards.prompt: Christopher Columbus reached the Americas in 1492.
cards.explanation: Columbus crossed the Atlantic and reached the Americas region in 1492.

cards.prompt: In which year did World War II end?
The storming of which fortress-prison symbolized the start of the French Revolution in 1789?
cards.explanation: The storming of the Bastille on 14 July 1789 symbolized the revolution's beginning.

cards.prompt: Which wall divided East and West Berlin and fell in 1989? (name it)
cards.explanation: The Berlin Wall divided the city from 1961 and fell in 1989, symbolizing the Cold War's end.

cards.prompt: Which ancient civilization built the pyramids at Giza? (name the civilization)
cards.explanation: The ancient Egyptians built the pyramids at Giza as royal tombs.
